title: End-to-end transaction processing with order management

requirements.text: Extend the backend order data model and actor to support full order lifecycle transitions: pending → processing → shipped → delivered → cancelled. Expose update endpoints so that order status can be changed programmatically, and persist all status changes in stable storage.

quotes: Order management: placement, tracking, status updates

acceptanceCriteria: Order status field supports at minimum: #pending, #processing, #shipped, #delivered, #cancelled variants.
A backend endpoint exists to update an order's status given an order ID and new status value.
Status changes are persisted across upgrades.
Only authorized callers (admin or the owning user for cancellation) can transition order status.

requirements.text: Implement end-to-end transaction processing in the backend: when a Stripe payment confirmation is received, atomically mark the associated order as #processing, record the confirmed payment details, and ensure the order cannot be double-confirmed.
acceptanceCriteria: Confirming a Stripe session ID transitions the linked order from #pending to #processing.
Re-confirming an already-confirmed session ID returns an error or a no-op result rather than updating the order again.
Payment session ID is stored on the order record after confirmation.
A query endpoint returns the full order details including payment status.

requirements.text: Add a React Query hook (useUpdateOrderStatus) and a backend call to allow admin users to manually move an order through its status stages from the Admin Dashboard.

quotes: Order management: placement, tracking, status updates

acceptanceCriteria: useUpdateOrderStatus mutation hook is defined in useQueries.ts and calls the backend status-update endpoint.
Admin Dashboard page includes an order management section listing all orders with their current status.
Each order row exposes a status selector or action buttons to advance/cancel the order status.
Successful status update reflects immediately in the UI via query invalidation.

requirements.text: Enhance the MyOrders page and OrderConfirmation page to display real-time order tracking information: current status badge, a visual status timeline (pending → processing → shipped → delivered), estimated stage labels, and a refresh/polling mechanism so status updates appear without a full page reload.

quotes: Order management: placement, tracking, status updates

acceptanceCriteria: MyOrders page shows a status badge for each order using the full status vocabulary (#pending, #processing, #shipped, #delivered, #cancelled).
OrderConfirmation page renders a horizontal or vertical status timeline that highlights the current stage.
Orders in active states (#pending, #processing, #shipped) are polled for status updates at a reasonable interval (e.g., every 30 seconds) using React Query's refetchInterval.
Cancelled orders are visually distinguished (e.g., muted or strikethrough style).

requirements.text: Update the PaymentSuccess page to call the payment confirmation endpoint after Stripe redirects back, update the linked order status to #processing, and display a confirmation summary with the updated order status before redirecting the user to the OrderConfirmation page.
acceptanceCriteria: On mount, PaymentSuccess page calls the confirmPayment backend endpoint with the Stripe session ID from the URL.
While confirming, a loading state is shown.
On success, the page displays a brief confirmation summary (order ID, status: Processing, item count, total) before navigating to /order/:id.
On error (e.g., already confirmed or session not found), a descriptive error message is shown with a link to MyOrders.

requirements.text: Generate a migration module (migration.mo) that upgrades existing stored order records to include the new status variants and any new fields (e.g., paymentSessionId, statusHistory) added in REQ-1 and REQ-2, defaulting missing fields to safe initial values.

quotes: Order management: placement, tracking, status updates

acceptanceCriteria: migration.mo transforms all existing order records to the new schema without data loss.
Orders that were previously paymentConfirmed=true are migrated to status #processing.
Orders without a prior payment confirmation default to status #pending.
All other order fields are passed through unchanged.

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo).
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Preserve existing Stripe checkout session creation flow; only extend it, do not replace it.
Role-based access control must be enforced: only admins can update any order status; users can only cancel their own orders.
nonGoals: Introducing a new payment provider or replacing Stripe.
Real-time WebSocket-based order updates (polling is acceptable).
Shipping carrier integration or live tracking numbers.
Email or push notification of status changes.
Changes to the vendor product management or approval workflow.
